ingredients.name: Maruko Bridge
ingredients.address: Maruko Bridge, Denenchofu, Nakahara Ward, Ota City, Tokyo 211-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive arch of Maruko Bridge over the Tama River appears in episode 3 as Tohru walks home along the riverbank, one of the recurring riverside backdrops that frames her daily commute and quiet moments of solitude.

ingredients.name: Setagaya Park
ingredients.localName: 世田谷区立世田谷公園
ingredients.address: Setagaya Park, 1 Chome-5-27 Ikejiri, Setagaya City, Tokyo 154-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Setagaya Park features in episode 22 as a backdrop for outdoor scenes near the Soma estate area, with its open lawns reflecting the quieter residential side of Tokyo that the story inhabits.

ingredients.name: Tamagawa Jiyu Hiroba
ingredients.entityContext: This riverside open space along the Tama River appears in episode 22 as a location near where characters spend time outdoors, part of the Komae/Izumi area cluster of pilgrimage spots tied to Tohru's neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Marukobashi Police Box
ingredients.address: Marukobashi Police Box, 1-chōme Marukodōri, Nakahara Ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa 211-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small police box near Maruko Bridge appears in episode 23, a background detail that grounds the show's depiction of the residential Kawasaki riverfront where several scenes along Tohru's route are set.

ingredients.name: Kashimapiano School
ingredients.entityContext: This local piano school near Izumi-Tamagawa appears in episode 21, part of the quiet residential streetscape the anime uses to depict the neighborhood surrounding the area where Tohru and her friends live.

ingredients.name: Izumi-Tamagawa Station
ingredients.address: Izumi-Tamagawa Station, 4-chōme-2 Higashiizumi, Komae, Tokyo 201-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Izumi-Tamagawa Station on the Odakyu Line serves as a key neighborhood landmark in episode 21, representing the modest commuter stop near Tohru's humble living situation before she moves in with the Somas.

ingredients.name: Senzokuike Park
ingredients.address: Senzokuike Park, 2 Chome-14-5 Minamisenzoku, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Senzokuike Park, with its scenic pond, appears in episode 20 as a tranquil outdoor setting, reflecting the quiet park scenery the 2019 series uses throughout its depiction of southern Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Murata Clinic
ingredients.address: Murata Hospital, 4 Chome-2-1 Tajima, Ikuno Ward, Osaka, 544-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Murata Clinic in Osaka appears in episode 20 as the reference location for a medical facility scene, though its Osaka coordinates stand out from the predominantly Tokyo/Kawasaki cluster of locations in the series.

ingredients.name: Maple Wood
ingredients.entityContext: This residential building in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 19 as part of the upscale neighborhood backdrop the anime uses for the Soma family's affluent surroundings in Ota City.

ingredients.name: GRANDIR Jiyugaoka
ingredients.address: GRANDIR Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome-15-4 Jiyugaoka, Meguro City, Tokyo 152-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This French bakery in Jiyugaoka appears in episode 19, its charming European-style shopfront serving as visual reference for the fashionable Jiyugaoka shopping district that appears in the background of the 2019 anime.

ingredients.name: Former Den-en-chofu Station Building
ingredients.address: Former Den-en-chofu Station Building, 3 Chome-25-18 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The beautiful 1923 octagonal station building at Den-en-chofu appears in episode 17 as a recognizable landmark in the wealthy residential district that the Soma family calls home, its elegant historical architecture a perfect visual match for the Soma estate's high-class setting.

ingredients.name: Tama River riverside dog run
ingredients.localName: 多摩川河川敷ドッグラン
ingredients.entityContext: The riverside dog run area along the Tama River near Komae appears in episode 9, part of the open riverbank scenery that the series uses for outdoor scenes near Tohru's neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Moto Izumi Sankaku Plaza
ingredients.entityContext: This small triangular plaza near Izumi-Tamagawa appears in episode 9, part of the residential Komae streetscape that the 2019 series used as background for scenes around the neighborhood where Tohru lives before the Soma household.

ingredients.name: Doutor Shinjuku 3-chome East Shop
ingredients.localName: ドトールコーヒーショップ 新宿３丁目東店
ingredients.address: Doutor Shinjuku 3-chome East Shop, 花菱ビル １Ｆ 3 Chome-11-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Doutor coffee shop in Shinjuku 3-chome appears in episode 9 as a background cafe location, part of the urban Tokyo scenery used when characters venture into the city center.

ingredients.name: Green Hills Denenchofu
ingredients.localName: グリーンヒルズ田園調布
ingredients.address: Green Hills Denenchofu, グリーンヒルズ田園調布 4 Chome-34-8 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This condominium in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 8 as part of the upscale residential streetscape surrounding the Soma household, the elegant tree-lined streets of Den-en-chofu providing the rich backdrop for Kyo and Yuki's school commute.

ingredients.name: View Denenchofu
ingredients.address: View Denenchofu, ビュー田園調布 4 Chome-33-3 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The View Denenchofu condominium complex appears in episode 8 alongside other Den-en-chofu residences, its placement in the affluent Ota City neighborhood reinforcing the Soma family's status in the series.

ingredients.name: Umasaka
ingredients.entityContext: This slope in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 6, one of the gently winding residential streets in the Soma neighborhood that Tohru, Kyo, and Yuki walk together during early episodes as Tohru settles into life with the Somas.

ingredients.name: Akiba Kuromatsu
ingredients.entityContext: The Akiba black pine, a notable old tree near Den-en-chofu, appears in episode 6 as a distinctive natural landmark along the residential streets where characters walk, a subtle nod to the historic character of the neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Heitai-ya
ingredients.address: Heitai-ya, 3 Chome-14-10 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This soba shop in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 5 as a background location in the residential neighborhood, part of the local commercial streetscape that grounds the Soma household's surroundings in everyday Tokyo life.

ingredients.name: Horai Park
ingredients.address: Hōrai Park, 3 Chome-31-1 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Horai Park in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 4, a quiet neighborhood park near the Soma household where early outdoor scenes take place as Tohru begins her new life living with the Soma family.

ingredients.name: Gashin Musashikosugi
ingredients.address: Gashin Musashikosugi, 3 Chome-269-2 Kosugimachi, Nakahara Ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa 211-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This izakaya in Musashi-Kosugi appears in episode 3, part of the commercial district near Maruko Bridge that the anime uses as background scenery for the Kawasaki side of the Tama River.

ingredients.name: Den-en-chofu Station
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒145-0071 Tokyo, Ota City, Den-en-chofu Station, 田園調布 3 丁目 25-18
ingredients.entityContext: Den-en-chofu Station, with its distinctive radial boulevard layout, appears in episode 10 as a key hub in the Soma neighborhood, the elegant station area immediately recognizable to fans as the heart of the upscale district where the Soma family lives.

ingredients.name: 109 Cinemas Futako-tamagawa
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒158-0094 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Tamagawa, 1 Chome−14−1 109 Cinemas Futako-tamagawa, 「二子玉川ライズ・ショッピングセンター・テラスマーケット」内
ingredients.entityContext: This cinema complex at Futako-tamagawa Rise appears in episode 10, featuring as a recognizable modern landmark when characters are seen in the bustling commercial district near the Tama River.

ingredients.name: Ertugrul
ingredients.localName: ケバブカフェ エルトゥールル
ingredients.entityContext: This kebab cafe in Nakano appears in episode 13 as a background location, part of the wider Tokyo urban scenery the series visits beyond the main Den-en-chofu neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Sumitomo Mitsui Bank, Musashi-Kosugi Branch

ingredients.address: Sumitomo Mitsui Bank, Musashi-Kosugi Branch, 3 Chome-1200 Shinmarukohigashi, Nakahara Ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa 211-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Sumitomo Mitsui Bank in Musashi-Kosugi appears in episode 14 as part of the commercial streetscape near the Kawasaki riverfront, a background detail in scenes set in the area around Maruko Bridge.

ingredients.name: Futako-tamagawa Rise Central Square
ingredients.localName: 二子玉川ライズ 中央広場
ingredients.address: Futako-tamagawa Rise Central Square, 1 Chome-14-1 Tamagawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 158-0094, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The central plaza of Futako-tamagawa Rise shopping complex appears in episode 14 as a modern commercial backdrop, one of the recognizable contemporary landmarks visible from the Tama River area near the Setagaya/Kawasaki border.

ingredients.name: Shibakoen Sta.(Bus)
ingredients.address: Shibakoen Sta., 2-chōme-6 Shibakōen, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shibakoen bus stop near Shiba Park appears in episode 14, part of the Minato City scenery used when characters travel to the Tokyo Tower area, a location distinct from the main Den-en-chofu cluster.

ingredients.name: Shibakoen Station.
ingredients.address: Shibakoen Sta., 4 Chome-8-14 Shibakoen, Minato City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shibakoen subway station near Shiba Park appears in episode 14, used as a transit landmark when scenes shift to the Minato City area near Tokyo Tower, one of the few locations outside the Ota/Kawasaki core of the show.

ingredients.name: Denenchofu Horai Residence
ingredients.localName: 田園調布宝来レジデンス
ingredients.address: Denenchofu Horai Residence, 田園調布宝来レジデンス 3 Chome-33-24 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This residential building in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 16 as part of the affluent neighborhood streetscape, its location in the Horai area of Den-en-chofu placing it close to the park and residential blocks the anime repeatedly depicts near the Soma household.

ingredients.name: Odakyu 2nd Parking
ingredients.localName: 小田急和泉多摩川第3駐車場
ingredients.address: Odakyu 2nd Parking, 4 Chome-11 Higashiizumi, Komae, Tokyo 201-0014, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Odakyu parking lot near Izumi-Tamagawa appears in episode 16 as part of the commuter infrastructure around the station that marks Tohru's original home territory before she moved in with the Somas.

ingredients.address: Sarashina Higashi-Tamagawa, 1 Chome-36-13 Higashitamagawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 158-0084, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This soba restaurant in Higashi-Tamagawa appears in episode 17, part of the local dining and commercial scenery in the residential Setagaya area east of the Soma neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Denenchofu Skyscraper
ingredients.address: Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This tall building in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 17 as a background landmark in the Soma neighborhood, part of the residential Ota City skyline visible during outdoor scenes near the Soma estate.

ingredients.name: Futaba Gakuen mae
ingredients.localName: 雙葉中学校・高等学校
ingredients.address: Futaba Junior & Senior High School, 14-1 Rokubancho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 102-0085, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Futaba Junior and Senior High School in Chiyoda appears in episode 17 as the real-world model for the girls' school referenced in the series, its prestigious Catholic school character fitting the high-status schools the Soma cousins attend in the anime.

ingredients.name: Shiba Park
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-8 Shibakoen, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shiba Park near Tokyo Tower appears in episode 14 as a green space location when characters visit the Minato City area, the park's proximity to the tower making it a visually distinctive stop outside the main Den-en-chofu pilgrimage cluster.

ingredients.name: Chiisaki Hanano Yochien
ingredients.address: Chiisaki Hanano Yōchien, 3 Chome-30-25 Denenchofu, Ota City, Tokyo 145-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Chiisaki Hanano Yochien kindergarten in Den-en-chofu appears in episode 1 as the early establishing backdrop for the Soma neighborhood, the charming school near Horai Park helping set the quiet, residential tone of the upscale area where Tohru's new life begins.
